question: Do CD-specific patterns produce different variance ratios than Canonical Six?
key_finding: CD-specific P-vectors are IDENTICAL to Canonical Six P-vectors. The framework distinction (CD vs Clifford) resides entirely in the Q-element (second factor), not the P-element. Poi/GUE and Act/GUE variance ratios are therefore identical between canonical and CD-specific patterns sharing the same P-vector.
antipodal_finding: CD-specific patterns CONTAIN an antipodal pair: CD2_pat58_P (v2) + CD3_pat81_P (v3) = 0. This is the same antipodal pair as the Canonical Six (v2+v3=0). The antipodal structure is shared between canonical and CD-specific patterns.

p_vector_overlap.CD1_pat19: shares P-vector v1 with Canonical Pat18
p_vector_overlap.CD2_pat58: shares P-vector v2 with Canonical Pat59
p_vector_overlap.CD3_pat81: shares P-vector v3 with Canonical Pat84
p_vector_overlap.CD4_pat101: shares P-vector v4 with Canonical Pat102
p_vector_overlap.CD5_pat103: shares P-vector v4 with Canonical Pat102 (identical to CD4)
p_vector_overlap.CD6_pat121: shares P-vector v5 with Canonical Pat124

verdict: Framework-independence is NOT the operative algebraic property for embed_pair projection measurements. The P-vector (first factor of the zero divisor pair) entirely determines the embed_pair projection result. CD-specific and canonical patterns sharing a P-vector produce identical results. The Clifford vs CD-only distinction resides in the Q-element, which embed_pair does not access. Implication: the measurement operates on the E8 Weyl orbit geometry of the P-vectors, not the full bilateral zero divisor structure.
structural_note_antipodal: The antipodal pair v2+v3=0 appears in BOTH canonical (Pat59/Pat84) and CD-specific (Pat58/Pat81) patterns. The antipodal structure is a property of the P-vector geometry (E8 Weyl orbit), not of framework-independence. This strengthens the E8 Weyl orbit geometry as the operative algebraic structure underlying all measurements.
implication_for_mechanism: The mechanism connecting the sedenion kernel to zero spectral structure operates through the E8 P-vector geometry, not through the full bilateral zero divisor product. The Clifford vs CD distinction (framework-independence) does not affect embed_pair projections. To probe the full bilateral structure, a measurement that accesses Q-vector information is needed — multi-channel embed or direct sedenion multiplication.
